$comment: The struct-body descriptor mechanism: <sys/time.h>'s `structs` surface declares `struct timeval {i64 tv_sec; i64 tv_usec}`; the semantic phase injects it (tag + field scope + compositeScopeByType) so by-name field access compiles and the layout engine derives offsets 0/8. exit 42 witnesses tag resolution + field access + correct offsets on the unix ABIs (elf x86-64 + arm64-qemu + macho). NO windows-pe arm: <sys/time.h> is POSIX, declared `availableObjectFormats:[elf,macho]`, so a pe compile FAILS LOUD (c8 per-target availability). RED-on-disable: without the structs surface + injection, `struct timeval` is unknown → compile fails. The release arm confirms the full shipped pipeline (the optimizer over the new struct GEP/Load shapes).
